name: Hon Thom Departure Terminal - Sun World Hon Thom Nature Park

reviews.author: Nanda Sundri
reviews.text: We had a wonderful morning at Sun World. We started our journey at the Hon Thom Cable Car Terminal, which was modern, clean, and efficiently managed. The terminal had comfortable waiting areas and offered stunning ocean views. The ticket included a complimentary beer, and the service staff at the restaurant and throughout the park were courteous and helpful. The washrooms were clean and well-maintained, and there were thousands of visitors a day. The cable car ride itself was spectacular. It was smooth, scenic, and offered panoramic views of the islands and the emerald-green waters below. It is the longest cable car ride according to the Guinness book of world record! There were on-site cafés and shops where we could relax and take in the surroundings. The staff were polite and attentive, ensuring a pleasant visit from start to finish. Sun World is a must-see destination for kids. The water park looks amazing, and there are many scenic spots that are perfect for taking photos. If you have time, visit the KISS Bridge around sunset for a truly unforgettable experience. Overall, it was an unforgettable afternoon and one of the highlights of our trip to Phu Quoc!

reviews.author: Manisha Suru
reviews.text: Visited Sun World Hon Thom Nature Park and overall it’s a solid half-day experience. The cable car is the highlight — stunning aerial views and a smooth 15–20 minute ride each way. Definitely worth doing at least once. Aquatopia Water Park is clean and fun, especially for families and kids, though not world-class if you’ve visited major international water parks. Food inside is average and slightly overpriced. The beach is nice for relaxing but not extraordinary. Best to go early morning or late afternoon to avoid heat and crowds. Overall: worth it mainly for the cable car and a fun family day out.

reviews.author: ahmad shukri che mamat
reviews.text: The Hon Thom Departure Terminal at Sun World Hon Thom Nature Park is an impressive gateway to one of Phú Quốc’s most iconic attractions. The terminal itself is clean, tidy, and beautifully maintained, creating a welcoming atmosphere right from the start. Despite the large number of visitors, the service remains smooth and efficient, ensuring that the experience is stress-free and well-organized. Stepping into the cable car, you’ll immediately notice the comfort and spaciousness. The ride is exceptionally smooth, allowing you to relax and enjoy the journey. As the cable car glides over the turquoise waters and lush islands, the panoramic views are simply breathtaking — a highlight that makes the trip unforgettable. From the spotless terminal to the seamless service and the magnificent cable car ride, Sun World Hon Thom Nature Park delivers an experience that feels world-class. It’s not just a mode of transport, but a destination in itself. Highly recommended for anyone visiting Phú Quốc.

reviews.author: Neha Kanagat
reviews.text: We spent a delightful afternoon at the Hon Thom Cable Car Terminal and thoroughly enjoyed the entire experience. We made the mistake of including this place in our island hopping tours whereas I feel it should be a full day spent here tour. The terminal is modern, clean, and efficiently run, offering comfortable waiting areas and great ocean views. The lunch buffet at Mango is mind boggling.They have a variety of cuisines meats, fruits, fresh juices and also a Goblet full of Beer free 😋 😀. The service staff at the restyrant and all over are courteous n helpful. Clean washrooms everywhere inspite of atleast 5 to 7k visitors a day The cable car journey itself was spectacular — smooth, scenic, and offering panoramic views of the islands and emerald-green waters below. The on-site cafés and shops added to the convenience, allowing us to relax and take in the surroundings. The staff were polite and attentive, ensuring a pleasant visit from start to finish. Overall, an unforgettable afternoon and one of the highlights of our trip to Phu Quoc!

reviews.author: Minh Van
reviews.text: Riding the Hon Thom Cable Car feels less like transportation and more like a quiet flight above paradise. The view is unreal — emerald sea below, scattered islands glowing under the sun, and fishing boats that look like tiny dots moving slowly through the blue. It’s the kind of sight that makes you forget to pull out your phone. The cabins are modern, smooth, and surprisingly quiet. You can feel the breeze even when the door is closed, and for a moment, it’s just you, the sky, and the sea. The whole ride lasts around 15 minutes, but honestly, it feels both too short and perfectly long at the same time. At the end of the ride, Hon Thom Island opens up with beaches, water slides, and palm-lined paths. It’s clearly designed for tourists — beautiful but a bit commercial — yet still worth the trip for the view alone. One small minus: queues can get long at peak hours, and staff sometimes seem overwhelmed. But once you’re up there, none of that matters much.

reviews.author: Alexis S
reviews.text: I would best describe this a the Las Vegas of Vietnam based in the artificial nature of this place. It is an Italy-theme park. This was worth the price but only just barely because it was under construction. Everything was new and very clean. When we went there were three rides and they were pretty good. We didn’t do the water park but it looked pretty good. The cable car itself was very good and well organised. I would go early and enjoy the water park if you are travelling with children.

reviews.author: adva krohn
reviews.text: The water park is a bit pricey, but totally worth it! There are plenty of slides and attractions, and the staff are very strict about safety rules, age limits, and proper swimwear. If your swim shorts have zippers, you won’t be allowed on certain slides — so keep that in mind. There’s a Starbucks right next to the park, though it’s also quite expensive and doesn’t have all the drinks you might expect. The nearby tuk-tuks are on the pricey side too. Access can be a little tricky, as there’s a pedestrian-only bridge, and you’ll need to find a place to park your scooter a bit further away. Despite that, the place is beautiful, peaceful, and very well kept — a lovely spot to spend the day and have fun in the water! 💦🌴☀️

reviews.author: marco thurau
reviews.text: Riding the world’s longest continuous cable car was a pleasant experience, but unfortunately that’s where the positives end. The island itself was a major disappointment. Almost everywhere you look, it feels like one huge construction site — noisy, chaotic, and with large parts of the natural environment being destroyed for no apparent reason. What could be a beautiful destination is instead overcrowded, loud, and completely lacking any authentic charm. Another major drawback is the pricing structure. There is no option to buy a ticket for the cable car only. Instead, you are required to pay 750,000 VND, which includes entry to the water park and one drink (beer or juice). This might be fine for people who enjoy spending their day in a heavily overcrowded water park, but for anyone simply wanting to experience the cable car ride, it feels both unnecessary and overpriced. The entire experience left me feeling that the place prioritizes profit over quality and sustainability. Between the crowds, the noise, the construction, and the inflated prices, it’s hard to find anything that would make me want to return. I would not visit again, and I cannot recommend it.

reviews.author: Diskon4no
reviews.text: Really super and special experience for everyone! Basically, you purchase an all inclusive ticket for the cable car and that gives you access to all the attractions on the island with the exception of food. We enjoyed the water park and the shows that pop up on the island. Our only issue was when we went to leave, so did everyone else! Took much longer than expected to board the cable car to return to the main island so plan accordingly!

reviews.author: Scott Miller
reviews.text: Well worth doing seeing how it is the Guinness book of world records for the longest tram ride, stunning views on your way to the island. Highly recommend doing the buffet as well so many food choices it was unreal. The theme park water park also extremely nice as well and the beach beautiful beyond. Highly recommend would definitely do it again. Staff extremely friendly and helpful with all your questions and needs.

reviews.author: Saneesh P.S
reviews.text: Hon Thom Park is a fun place with a mix of water rides, beach views, and island vibes. The water park is well-maintained and enjoyable for both adults and kids. The cable car ride to reach the park is a highlight with stunning sea views. It can get crowded during peak hours, but overall it’s a good spot to spend a relaxed and playful day in Phu Quoc.

reviews.author: Sandeep Gaikwad -Sandy
reviews.text: It's a beautiful place to visit. The cable car ride is around 8 kilometer long but it takes hardly 15-20 minutes to reach. The journey is beautiful as you ride above the sea making it memorable. There is also an adventure park or a theme park when you get down. There is also an Aqua theme park where you can enjoy different rides. When you return back to the departure terminal you can enjoy the kiss of the sea show which is at around 19:45 hrs.

reviews.author: Victorya
reviews.text: I very much recommend taking the cable car! It takes you to sun world where there is a water park, a beach and a small amusement park. The rides at the amusement park open much later (opening times in the picture). The beach opens at 9:30. There is a free shuttle that will take you there. If you want a sun bed (also free) make sure to get there early! Note that they are still building many parts of this island so there will be construction and empty buildings but the cable car alone is already worth it! Also FYI from 11:30-13:30 the cable car doesn't run ans each way is approximately 20 minutes. When going up you need to also account for the waiting time.

reviews.author: WhoKnown19 Player
reviews.text: I went quite late for the cable car ride, so it ended up being just me and my friend in the whole cabin. It actually created such a nice, private atmosphere The ride was long, but the views were amazing. Even though we didn’t have time to visit other spots afterward (and we didn’t really mind), we were already happy with the beautiful scenery we got to enjoy.

reviews.author: Med E
reviews.text: We had a wonderful time visiting Thom Islet, also known as Hon Thom Island, part of the Sun World complex in Phu Quoc. The trip starts with an amazing cable car ride — one of the longest in the world — offering breathtaking panoramic views over the ocean and small islands below. Once on the island, the atmosphere is vibrant and well-maintained. The aqua park is a highlight, perfect for families and adults alike, with clean facilities and plenty of fun slides. The beaches are stunning, with crystal-clear waters and soft white sand ideal for relaxing after a few activities. There are many photo spots, beach clubs, and restaurants, all beautifully designed and managed. The staff are polite and professional, ensuring everything runs smoothly. It’s best to spend half a day to a full day here to really enjoy all the attractions. A great mix of adventure, leisure, and natural beauty — definitely a must-visit when in Phu Quoc!

reviews.author: R R L
reviews.text: If you’re visiting Phu Quoc, the Sunset Town and the cable car ride are must-do experiences. The cable car package includes entry to the island, a water park, and some other activities, which I found less impressive. But the cable car ride itself is absolutely mesmerizing. If you’re planning a trip to Phu Quoc, don’t rely on photos or videos—see it with your own eyes. It’s truly a once-in-a-lifetime experience, and the views are nothing short of breathtaking.

reviews.author: ChandraNag B S
reviews.text: Our visit to sun World HonThom island was truly an unforgettable experience.This incredible attraction holds the record for being the world's longest cable car 🚠 nearly 8kms , streching over the turquoise sea that connects phu quoc to hon thom island. We booked our entry tickets through the klook app,which mad ethe entire process super convenient.All we had to do was scan the QR code at the entrance and we were good to go,no long queues or hassles.One can also get the entry tickets in the counters. The cable car journey takes around 20 minutes, and honestly, it’s a breathtaking ride. The sea view from above is absolutely unmatchable—crystal-clear waters, scattered fishing boats, and tiny islands below create a truly mesmerizing sight. It felt like floating through paradise, and we loved every bit of it. Once you reach the Hon Thom island ,you’re greeted with multiple photo points that are perfect for capturing those postcard-worthy moments. By default, your ticket also includes access to the two theme parks on the island ~one with water games and another with dry games. At the time of our visit, the dry games section was under renovation, so not many rides were open. However, we spent most of our time enjoying the water park, which had several fun slides and a relaxing lazy pool. The only downside was that there’s no lift access to the slides, so you’ll need to climb 100–200 steps for most rides, which can get quite tiring. Another disappointing part was the locker and shower facilities.The lockers cost 100000VND each ,which felt a bit expensive and the shower area wasn't very well maintained , cleanliness could be definitely improved. But all those minor inconveniences were forgotten when we took the cable car back during sunset. Watching the sun dip into the ocean, with speedboats and fishing boats returning to the mainland, was one of the most magical moments of our trip. The entire sky turned golden-orange, and the view from the moving cable car was simply spectacular. Don’t forget to claim your complimentary crafted beer (included with your ticket)—just ask any staff member, and they’ll guide you where to get it. And if you’re staying late, make sure to catch the “Kiss the Stars” bridge show (entry ticket required). It’s a stunning night spectacle worth seeing! Overall it was a perfect blend of adventure , natural and man made beauty with unforgettable memories. Despite a few areas that could use improvement , the cable car ride alone makes it totally worth visiting

reviews.author: Kevin Häfeli
reviews.text: Undecided about this. Its actually a theme park city or partly a ghost town. The facilities are well maintained, modern and clean. However, it's very touristic and I guess a great place for families. As a tourist enjoying nature, you can do the long cable 🚠 and see the islands.

reviews.author: Swatantra Khandelwal
reviews.text: We arrived around 15:50 at the cable car terminal at Hon Thon island well in advance. The cable car service closes at 17:00. So the line was relatively short and the wait time was around 15 min. We boarded the cable car at 16:10 and as the cable car was not crowded we were able to enjoy nicely.

reviews.author: 360 MAGAZINE
reviews.text: The Hon Thom Cable Car travels 4.9 miles over several islands to a theme park with water rides, restaurants, beautiful resorts, and breathtaking beaches. You can book a ride at the southernmost tip of Phu Quoc's Suntown in Vietnam. The panoramic views were so magnificent that we were literally hypnotized.

reviews.author: Time2bookatrip. com
reviews.text: This is one of the most amazing cable car route you can do in your life you would fly above beautiful Clearwater islands and reach the haunted island with the waterpark and the amusement park of sunworld. The cable car is 8 km long and starting from the fake Colosseum of Sunset town. The cabins are up to 30 people and don’t be scared if the cable car is stopping it will be only for five minutes. Taking count that the cable car is not working at noon from 11:30pm to 1:30 pm

reviews.author: Ales Bacmanak
reviews.text: This was probably the worst experience of my life. The cable car stopped after about 15 minutes of riding. There was only an announcement that the cable car was temporarily suspended. Can you imagine the feeling of helplessness at a height of about 60 m above sea level? After a technical repair, we finally arrived at the island where there were a lot of tourists. Completely crowded. Another shock came at lunch where it was literally a massacre because lunch was served buffet style. Head on head. However, we spent the whole day on a quiet beach. You have to consider whether it is worth about 30€ per person. We went there in the morning at 9 o'clock. Only the views of the island and sea were amazing.

reviews.author: sumeet mirgh
reviews.text: This is sunworld's creation which has an 8 km long cable car. The scenery is beautiful and the ride was safe. It takes approx 20 mins to reach the other side which has water sports and restaurants / crafted beer outlets. Runs between 9am to 12noon and then 1.30pm to 5pm all days of the week. Tickets cost 300,000 VND per person which includes entry to Kiss of the bridge spot.

reviews.author: M D
reviews.text: Epic views! We were lucky to get a cable car 1 way with just 1 couple but on way back full and lots of smelly people. Went and did cable car only not interested in waterpark or the island.

reviews.author: Aaron Lim
reviews.text: Got the tickets from klook in case the ticket office was crowded. Easy to find as there are landmarks and the cable car moving to see where is the station. Do go early as the ride drops u off at the amusement park where you can spend the whole day with activities. Lots of food options at reasonable price in the island. Time your exit well as the ride back could have the whole amusement park leaving at the same time.

reviews.author: Alyssa James
reviews.text: My review is for the cable car. It was beautiful being able to see the views of the island from so high up. Very serene. The ride went by a lot quicker than I thought it would, and I enjoyed every minute of it. Would highly recommend.

reviews.author: Daria Tsyba
reviews.text: Nice place but overcrowded. The road from northern part of phu quoc took ~2 hours by vinbus and free shuttle bus. I'd recommend to visit cable car in the morning to go to the island and come back at 15:30-16:00 to avoid long lines. Cable car route is amazing - long, with breathtaking view, comfortable. I didn't like amusement park and water park as well as beach - too many people, old construction, awful navigation, not tasty food. Be prepared to grab taxi in the evening - free shuttle bus doesn't operate after cable car station closure.

reviews.author: Valenciuc Alexandru Viorel
reviews.text: Nice touristy place. Buy 1 ticket for the cable car and you have some attractions included at the other end. We'll worth the money. Staff is very gentle and helpful 😊 🙏Thank you BTW.

reviews.author: Tây Noa
reviews.text: Our stay at the Long Beach Resort was wonderful. We really appreciated the unique, historic architecture and the beautiful beachfront location. It offers a very relaxing and distinct atmosphere, and the pools were great. It’s definitely a high-quality place if you want a calm retreat with character. To mix up the relaxation, we highly recommend the food tour with Moto Nomad Phu Quoc. This was the perfect balance to the resort stay! Being guided by locals on the back of a motorbike to hidden spots allowed us to sample truly authentic street food and see the island’s pulse. It was safe, fun, and the food was unbelievable. For anyone staying at Long Beach Resort, make sure to carve out time for the Moto Nomad food tour—it’s the best way to connect with the island's authentic culinary side.

reviews.author: Akshay Dimri
reviews.text: The cable car is a must do experience. Water park is good for all age groups. Lots of rider were under maintenance and some areas were under construction.
